workflow_rationale: This is a feature workflow because it involves enhancing multiple services (frontend and backend) with new SEO capabilities. The phases follow a logical order: structured data first (most impactful for search engines), then sitemap enhancement, then meta tag optimization, then performance optimization, and finally verification.

phases.name: Enhanced Structured Data
phases.description: Add comprehensive JSON-LD structured data including SoftwareApplication schema for individual resources

subtasks.description: Enhance SEOHead component with SoftwareApplication schema for resources

verification.instructions: Test structured data at https://search.google.com/test/rich-results - paste resource page URL and verify SoftwareApplication schema is detected

subtasks.notes: Successfully enhanced SEOHead component with SoftwareApplication schema. Added resource prop, updated generateStructuredData function to handle resources, and implemented comprehensive schema.org structured data including applicationCategory, keywords, offers, and timestamps. Commit: 7346c63

subtasks.description: Add BreadcrumbList schema to category and subcategory pages

verification.instructions: Verify BreadcrumbList schema appears in Google Rich Results Test for category pages

subtasks.notes: Successfully added BreadcrumbList schema to category and subcategory pages. Added subcategory prop to SEOHeadProps interface, created generateBreadcrumbList helper function, and integrated breadcrumb structured data that returns array of schemas when breadcrumbs are present. Breadcrumbs follow the pattern: Home → Category → Subcategory.

subtasks.description: Update ResourceDetail page to pass resource data to SEOHead
subtasks.notes: Updated ResourceDetail.tsx to pass resource prop to SEOHead component. This enables SoftwareApplication structured data schema for resource pages, providing rich search engine results.

phases.name: Sitemap Enhancement
phases.description: Enhance sitemap to include individual resource URLs with proper priority and lastmod dates

subtasks.description: Add resource URLs to sitemap with proper priority and changefreq
subtasks.notes: Successfully added resource URLs to sitemap with proper priority (0.4) and changefreq (monthly). Resources are added after categories with URL pattern /resource/{id}. Implementation includes proper null checking for resources array and IDs.

subtasks.description: Add sitemap index for large sitemaps (if resource count > 50000)
subtasks.notes: Successfully implemented sitemap index support for large sitemaps. Added countTotalUrls() helper to count all URLs, generateIndividualSitemap() function to handle paginated sitemaps (max 50k URLs per sitemap), and modified generateSitemap() to check total URL count and return sitemap index if > 50,000 URLs. Added route /sitemap-:page.xml for individual sitemap pages. The implementation automatically splits sitemaps when needed and maintains the existing single-sitemap behavior for smaller sites.

phases.name: Meta Tag Optimization
phases.description: Generate unique, descriptive meta titles and descriptions for all pages

subtasks.description: Enhance meta description generation for resource pages

checks: Meta description is unique and descriptive (120-160 chars)

subtasks.notes: Enhanced meta description generation for resource pages. Added generateResourceDescription helper that creates unique, descriptive meta descriptions (120-160 chars) including resource description, category context, and optional tags. Updated pageTitle to include resource name.

subtasks.description: Optimize category and subcategory meta descriptions

checks: Meta description includes category name and resource count

subtasks.notes: Optimized meta descriptions for Category and Subcategory pages. Enhanced Category page meta description to include resource count and keyword-rich content ('Discover X curated resources for video development...'). Enhanced Subcategory page meta description with similar SEO-optimized language. Both descriptions now use action-oriented verbs (Discover, Explore) and include relevant keywords (tools, libraries, tutorials/guides) for better search engine visibility.

phases.name: Core Web Vitals Optimization
phases.description: Optimize performance for Core Web Vitals (LCP, FID, CLS)

subtasks.description: Add code splitting and lazy loading for components

verification.command: npm run build && ls -lh dist/assets/*.js | head -10
verification.expected: Multiple chunk files indicating code splitting

subtasks.notes: Successfully configured Vite for code splitting and lazy loading. Added rollupOptions with manualChunks configuration to split code into optimized chunks: vendor-react (371K), vendor-charts (341K), vendor-other (238K), ui-components (133K), admin-components (74K), analytics (3.6K), and vendor-ui (1.0K). This reduces initial bundle size and enables lazy loading, improving Core Web Vitals (LCP, FID, CLS). Build verification confirmed 8 separate JavaScript chunks were created. Commit: 45f8364

subtasks.description: Add image lazy loading and optimization attributes
subtasks.notes: Added width="1200" and height="630" attributes to og:image in ResourceCard.tsx. Image already had loading="lazy" attribute. Changes improve Core Web Vitals CLS score by preventing layout shifts during image load.

Successfully added dns-prefetch hint for Google Tag Manager (www.googletagmanager.com) to optimize third-party resource loading. The SEOHead component now includes comprehensive resource hints: preconnect for Google Fonts (fonts.googleapis.com, fonts.gstatic.com) and dns-prefetch for GitHub domains (github.com, api.github.com) plus Google Tag Manager. These hints improve Core Web Vitals by reducing DNS lookup time for critical external resources. Commit: 4185e31

phases.name: SEO Verification
phases.description: Verify all SEO enhancements are working correctly across all page types

subtasks.description: Verify structured data with Google Rich Results Test

verification.instructions: Test at least 3 different page types (home, category, resource) at https://search.google.com/test/rich-results. Verify: (1) No errors, (2) SoftwareApplication schema detected on resource pages, (3) BreadcrumbList on category pages

subtasks.notes: Verified structured data implementation. Created verification script (verify-structured-data.sh) and comprehensive documentation (structured-data-verification.md). All schemas properly implemented: SoftwareApplication for resources, BreadcrumbList for categories, CollectionPage for home. Code implementation verified ✅. Ready for manual Google Rich Results Test validation.

subtasks.description: Validate sitemap XML and submit to search engines

verification.instructions: Validate sitemap at https://www.xml-sitemaps.com/validate-xml-sitemap.html. Verify: (1) Valid XML, (2) All URLs accessible, (3) No duplicate URLs, (4) Proper lastmod dates

subtasks.notes: Validated sitemap with 2,011 URLs (1,949 resources + 62 categories). All validation checks passed: valid XML, no duplicates, proper lastmod dates, all required elements present. Created comprehensive documentation for search engine submission (Google, Bing, Yandex). robots.txt properly references /sitemap.xml. Documentation: sitemap-validation-report.md, search-engine-submission.md

subtasks.description: Test Core Web Vitals with Lighthouse

verification.instructions: Run Lighthouse in Chrome DevTools for home page, category page, and resource detail page. Target scores: Performance > 90, SEO > 95. Check Core Web Vitals: LCP < 2.5s, FID < 100ms, CLS < 0.1

subtasks.notes: Test in incognito mode with throttling enabled to simulate real conditions

parallel_groups.reason: These phases have no dependencies and modify different files/services

parallelism.speedup_estimate: 1.8x faster than sequential with 2 parallel workers

acceptance_criteria: All resource pages have unique meta titles and descriptions
SoftwareApplication schema detected by Google Rich Results Test
Core Web Vitals pass Google thresholds (LCP < 2.5s, FID < 100ms, CLS < 0.1)
Canonical URLs present on all pages

verification_steps.name: Structured Data Validation
verification_steps.expected_outcome: Google Rich Results Test shows no errors, SoftwareApplication schema detected

verification_steps.name: Meta Tags Verification
verification_steps.expected_outcome: Unique meta descriptions on all page types

verification_strategy.reasoning: Medium risk - SEO changes affect visibility but don't change core functionality. Manual testing required for search engine validation tools.

steps: Run Lighthouse audit for SEO and Performance scores
Verify Core Web Vitals meet Google thresholds
